ops.title: When a documented CLI flag fails, verify binary identity + upstream history before designing a workaround; and skills get no preflight/health-check steps
ops.body:
When a recipe's CLI flag fails, the user wants the *why* nailed down before any workaround: which binary resolved (`which -a`), what upstream ever supported (changelog/npm history/repo search), whether a newer version fixes it. ("check why state doesnt exitt anymore. make sure its using /opt/homebrew/bin/agent-browser not some other one, check upstream, etc")

Separately: no preflight/health-check steps in skills — "this is wasteful we dont need this, dont add it" (re adding `cookiesync doctor` to the abwc skill's prereqs).

**Why:** the workaround differs completely depending on root cause (wrong binary vs regression vs never-existed), and preflight ceremony burns a command on every run for a failure that self-reports anyway.

**How to apply:** before rewriting a broken recipe, produce the binary-identity + upstream-history evidence; write prereqs so failures self-report instead of adding check steps. See [[agent-browser-state-handoff]], [[docs-terse-no-ceremony]].
